item.title: N.Y. House primaries test Mamdani's influence, and more races to watch today
item.description: Voters are going to the polls Tuesday for contests in New York, South Carolina, Maryland and Utah.

item.title: AI on pace to bypass cybersecurity systems soon, "Five Eyes" spy partners warn
item.description: The most advanced artificial intelligence models are improving quickly enough to outsmart prevailing cybersecurity know-how within months, the Five Eyes spy agency alliance is warning.

item.title: Medicare's AI push snarls patients and doctors in errors and delays
item.description: Medicare is testing the use of artificial intelligence to preapprove several healthcare services.

item.title: Iran to let nuclear inspections resume as talks continue, Vance says
item.description: Vance says Iran agreed to let international nuclear inspectors back into the country during a "very, very good" first day of negotiations with the U.S.

item.title: 2 killed, child injured in shooting at Chico library, police say
item.description: Two people were killed and a child was injured in a shooting inside a library in Chico on Monday, officials said.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Lionel Messi breaks record for most career World Cup goals
item.description: Lionel Messi made history Monday as he scored the 17th and 18th goals of his World Cup career, a new record. Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Former Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: Alan Greenspan was one of the longest-serving Federal Reserve chairs in U.S. history, steering the organization for 18 years under four presidents. Lesley Stahl spoke with Greenspan in 2007, more than a year after he retired. The former Fed chair died today at the age of 100.

item.title: 2 killed, including police officer, in shooting in Montreal
item.description: The suspect was shot and killed "right away," according to police, and there was no immediate word on a possible motive.

item.title: Clive Davis, legendary music executive, dies at age 94
item.description: Clive Davis helped shape the careers of music stars including Janis Joplin, Bruce Springsteen and Whitney Houston.

item.title: Children's book goes viral on TikTok, 40 years after being self-published
item.description: More than 40 years ago, a group of English professors at the University of Colorado wrote a children's book called "The Weighty Word Book." After a recent viral video post, the book sold more in a week than it had in nearly two decades. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: Remembering the life and legacy of Clive Davis
item.description: Clive Davis, the legendary music mogul who shaped the careers of several superstars, died Monday at 94. Carter Evans looks back on his life.

item.title: Trump says proof that vandals cut Reflecting Pool will be provided in court
item.description: President Trump has insisted that vandals, rather than questionable craftsmanship, are responsible for the enduring problems following the Reflecting Pool's $14.7 million sealant job.

item.title: Trump-backed populist wins close Colombia presidential runoff election
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, a Trump-backed conservative candidate who has never held public office, narrowly won Colombia's presidential runoff election Sunday. CBS News correspondent Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Trump administration planning to increase citizenship application fees
item.description: The Trump administration has unveiled a plan to make it more expensive for immigrants to become U.S. citizens. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the details.

item.title: Former Kevin McCarthy aide gives inside look at chaotic 2023 speaker race in new book
item.description: In January 2023, the House elected Kevin McCarthy as speaker after a historic and chaotic 15 roll-call votes. Former top McCarthy aide John Leganski looks back on that in his new memoir, "Glory, Grief, and the Gavel." Leganski joins "The Takeout" with more.

item.title: Iowa governor candidate Rob Sand dismisses opponent's attack: "He's mostly been living in Kansas"
item.description: It's been more than 15 years since a Democrat occupied the governor's mansion in Iowa. State Auditor Rob Sand is looking to change that. Sand joins "The Takeout" to discuss his gubernatorial campaign and attacks from his Republican opponent, Zach Lahn.

item.title: New York congressional candidate Alex Bores lays out his plans to regulate AI
item.description: New York City Democrats will head to the polls on Tuesday to choose who they want to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler. New York State Assembly Member Alex Bores, who is a part of the crowded primary field, joins "The Takeout" to discuss his priorities if elected to Congress.

item.title: 6/22: CBS Evening News
item.description: 2 killed, including police officer, in Montreal shooting; Nancy Guthrie's abductor likely sent ransom note saying she died, investigators believe.

item.title: Coke and Pepsi rolling out QR codes linked to ingredient information
item.description: The QR codes will take soda drinkers to a website listing more than 140 beverage ingredients and their nutritional content.

item.title: Company owned by Trump donor won $1.7 million no-bid Reflecting Pool contract
item.description: The federal government awarded a company owned by a Trump donor $1.7 million to install a new water cleaning system for the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, records show.

item.title: 6/22: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Vice President JD Vance says he feels great about progress on Iran talks; British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces his resignation.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration's database of Americans' personal info
item.description: U.S. District Judge Sparkle Sooknanan said the administration violated the law when it created a centralized database of Americans' personal records.

item.title: Polymarket launches probe after report alleges deceptive marketing
item.description: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that the prediction market paid content creators to produce videos of fake trades purporting to show big financial gains.

item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae bloom turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: Trump says he'll resurface Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, turning it blue
item.description: President Trump's renovation kick has now reached the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.

item.title: "Arc de Trump" receives preliminary design approval from arts commission
item.description: The plans call for the arch to be built on Columbia Island, a man-made strip of land in the Potomac River that is part of Washington, D.C.

item.title: Planned "Arc de Trump" would be over twice as high as Lincoln Memorial
item.description: Plans submitted by the Interior Department show the triumphal arch would be 250 feet tall, the tallest triumphal arch in the world.

item.title: Trump says he wants proposed arch to be world's "biggest"
item.description: The proposed site is situated along a flight path for nearby Reagan National Airport in Arlington, Virginia.

item.title: Arc de Trump? President shows off model of Independence Arch
item.description: President Trump waved a model of an arch that he wants to have built at the ceremonial entrance to Washington, D.C., by the nation's 250th anniversary next year.
